name: Crawfish Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chopped bell pepper, onion, and celery. Cook until vegetables are tender.
2. Add crawfish fat (if using) and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
3. Stir in the cooked crawfish tails, green onions, parsley, salt, black pepper, red pepper flakes, and garlic powder. Simmer gently for about 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
4. If desired, stir in a small amount of cornstarch dissolved in cold water to help thicken the sauce, if necessary.
5. Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
6. Place half of the prepared pie dough into a greased or lined 9-inch pie pan. Press it gently to fit and trim any excess.
7. Pour the cooked crawfish mixture over the bottom crust. Spread evenly.
8. Cover with the remaining dough, sealing the edges by pressing them together or crimping with fork tines.
9. Make small slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape during baking.
10. Bake for 10 minutes at 450°F (230°C). Then reduce the temperature to 375°F (190°C) and bake for an additional 35 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
Note: Individual pies can be made using a muffin pan with large cups. Follow the same steps as above.
